target.title: fix(codex): recover raw missing-thread compaction failures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87736.

This is a narrow follow-up to #86211 / #86602. The existing recovery path handles structured Codex native harness binding failures when `failure.reason` is `missing_thread_binding` or `stale_thread_binding`. A live regression on OpenClaw `2026.5.27` still surfaced the raw preflight error:

```text
Preflight compaction required but failed: thread not found: <codex-thread-id>
```

This patch classifies the raw `reason` text for the same missing/stale binding shapes as recoverable too, so preflight compaction continues into the existing recovery path instead of surfacing a generic Telegram failure.

## User-visible problem

A Telegram group inbound was accepted, but dispatch failed before a normal assistant turn. The user saw the generic channel failure message rather than automatic session recovery. The session later rotated/recovered and subsequent dispatches worked, which matches the same operational class as #86211.

All issue/PR evidence is sanitized: no private chat ids, message ids, session ids, bot handles, or raw Codex thread ids.

## Implementation

- Adds `isRecoverableNativeHarnessBindingReason()` to classify both structured binding reasons and raw missing-thread reason strings.
- Expands `isRecoverableNativeHarnessBindingFailure()` to inspect both `failure.reason` and top-level `reason`.
- Adds a regression test for `ok:false, compacted:false, reason:"thread not found: <codex-thread-id>"`.
- Keeps `live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts` unit-scoped by using a neutral `dynamic-fixture` provider instead of `opencode-go`, which can invoke real provider plugin hooks and repeatedly timed out in CI while validating this PR.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Raw missing-thread preflight compaction results should be treated as recoverable, matching the structured `missing_thread_binding` / `stale_thread_binding` recovery path from #86602.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout for this PR branch on Linux with Node `22.22.2`, using the real `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` runtime helper from `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local Node/tsx runtime invocation that created a redacted Telegram-group session entry, invoked `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded`, and returned a raw compaction result shaped as `{ ok:false, compacted:false, reason:"thread not found: <codex-thread-id>" }`.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "proof": "raw thread-not-found preflight compaction is recoverable",
  "returnedSameSessionEntry": true,
  "compactCalls": 1,
  "incrementCalls": 0,
  "threw": false
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The runtime helper returned the active session entry and did not throw `Preflight compaction required but failed: thread not found: <codex-thread-id>`.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram delivery against a production gateway running this branch was not tested; the proof covers the runtime recovery branch directly with sanitized local session state.

## Validation

Passed:

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
git diff --check

CI follow-up while validating:
- `checks-node-agentic-agents` repeatedly timed out in `src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts` on the `opencode-go` fixture path.
- The fixture now uses `dynamic-fixture` so the unit test does not enter real provider plugin hooks.
```

Attempted but not completed locally:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts -t 'unstructured thread-not-found'
```

The Vitest process stayed CPU-bound without reporter output in this fresh worktree; I stopped it rather than leave a long-running local process. The regression test is included for CI to exercise.
target.author: pfrederiksen

target.title: test(agents): add small model live profile
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODELS=small` for the direct live model matrix, covering constrained Qwen/GLM routes without expanding the modern profile.
- Reuses the curated-priority selection path for the small profile and passes the small refs into dynamic provider materialization.
- Documents the direct small profile plus the separate Ollama Cloud live smoke.

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.artifacts/vitest-cache/small-model-live-$USER-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-compat.test.ts src/agents/live-model-filter.test.ts src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-transport.test.ts` - 5 files / 139 tests passed on `1c42bf3c28`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/help/testing-live.md`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/help/testing-live.md`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/live-model-filter.ts src/agents/model-compat.test.ts src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/live-model-filter.ts src/agents/model-compat.test.ts src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- Previous Ollama Cloud live smoke: `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_BASE_URL=https://ollama.com OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_MODEL=glm-5.1:cloud OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_WEB_SEARCH=0 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_EMBEDDINGS=0 node scripts/test-live.mjs -- extensions/ollama/ollama.live.test.ts`
- Previous Crabbox AWS changed gate on the same patch shape: `run_83fe159a34d9`, lease `cbx_c700d99e4cc8`, slug `pearl-krill`, `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0. Current rebased SHA has focused proof; a fresh broad gate is waiting for current-main type gate unblocker #87750.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Maintainers can run a named direct small-model live profile instead of maintaining one-off comma lists for constrained Qwen/GLM coverage.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest/docs/lint after rebase, previous live Ollama Cloud API with `glm-5.1:cloud`, and previous AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate for the same patch shape.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Commands listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed; dynamic materialization now receives small refs; previous Ollama Cloud live smoke passed with 2 tests passed and 2 skipped; previous Crabbox `run_83fe159a34d9` exited 0; autoreview clean.

Observed result after fix: `OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODELS=small` is parsed as a curated direct profile and selected with small-profile priority/capping, including dynamic small OpenRouter/Z.AI/Qwen refs.

What was not tested: Direct `OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODELS=small` against OpenRouter/Z.AI/Qwen API keys in this environment; previous live proof covered hosted Ollama with `OLLAMA_API_KEY`; fresh broad changed gate on latest SHA until #87750 lands.
